seo.title: LaunchRefined — Luis Rojas
seo.description: Product refinement for validated MVPs built with Base44, Lovable, Bolt, v0, and similar tools. Audits, sprints, and upgrades—sharper UX, performance, and trust.

hero.eyebrow: AI app refinement for serious launches
hero.title: Your Base44 or Lovable MVP works. Now make it feel credible.
hero.description1: AI tools can get you to a working product fast. What they usually do not give you is product judgment: the clarity, polish, speed, and trust signals that make users stay, understand, and convert.
hero.description2: I help founders refine AI-built apps into products that feel intentional, usable, fast, and ready to be seen.
hero.visualCaption: Before → after: wireframe to production polish
hero.imageAlt: Illustration of refined product UI: layered dashboard mockups suggesting a polished, credible app after refinement

gap.title: The gap between “it works” and “it feels ready”
gap.description: Most AI-generated apps are functional enough to demo, but still fall short in the places that matter most once real users arrive.

genericUx.title: Generic UX
genericUx.description: The app technically works, but the experience feels templated, inconsistent, or forgettable.

weakHierarchy.title: Weak product hierarchy
weakHierarchy.description: Important actions do not stand out, screens compete for attention, and users are left guessing what matters.

trustFriction.title: Trust friction
trustFriction.description: Small details signal risk: awkward spacing, inconsistent copy, broken states, and flows that feel unfinished.

conversionLeakage.title: Conversion leakage
conversionLeakage.description: Users drop off because the product feels unclear, weak, slow, or not credible enough.

performanceFriction.title: Performance drag
performanceFriction.description: Slow loads, laggy interactions, and clunky mobile behavior make the product feel fragile and reduce confidence fast.

refinement.title: Refinement, not a rebuild
refinement.description: You do not always need to throw away the AI-generated version and start over. In many cases, the fastest path is to keep what is already working and improve the parts that affect perception, usability, speed, and conversion.

uiPolish.title: UI polish
uiPolish.description: Sharpen spacing, hierarchy, visual consistency, and states so the product feels designed instead of assembled.

uxClarity.title: UX clarity
uxClarity.description: Make flows easier to follow, reduce hesitation, and help users understand what to do next.

flowOptimization.title: Flow optimization
flowOptimization.description: Remove unnecessary friction from onboarding, form experiences, and key conversion paths.

performance.title: Performance and responsiveness
performance.description: Improve perceived speed, interaction smoothness, and responsiveness in the moments that affect trust most.

productionCleanup.title: Production-minded cleanup
productionCleanup.description: Address edge cases, rough interactions, loading states, and the details that break trust during real usage.

who.title: Who this is for
who.description: LaunchRefined is an exclusive service for founders, operators, and small teams who moved quickly with tools like Base44, Lovable, Bolt, or v0 and now need the product to hold up in front of customers, investors, or internal stakeholders.

bullets.mvp: You already have an MVP or working prototype
bullets.premium: You want the product to feel more premium and trustworthy
bullets.focused: You need focused refinement, not months of redesign work
bullets.cares: You care about both visual quality and functional UX
bullets.readiness: You want better performance, smoother interactions, and stronger conversion readiness

items.complete: Core actions become easier to complete
items.trustworthy: The app looks more trustworthy in demos and live use
items.fasterSmoother: The experience feels faster and smoother
items.aiSpeed: You keep the speed of AI tooling, but reduce the cost of rough execution

audit.label: Audit
audit.title: MVP Polish Audit
audit.description: A focused review of your current product with clear recommendations on UX, interface quality, performance issues, trust gaps, and conversion blockers.
audit.bestFor: Best for teams who want clear direction before investing in changes
included: Product review of key screens and flows
UX, UI, performance, and trust analysis

sprint.label: Sprint
sprint.title: Product Refinement Sprint
sprint.description: A short, high-impact engagement to improve the screens, flows, and details that make the product feel more polished, usable, fast, and launch-ready.
sprint.bestFor: Best for teams who need tangible improvements fast
included: Loading, empty, and error state improvements
Fixes for the most visible trust-breaking issues

upgrade.label: Upgrade
upgrade.title: Validated MVP Upgrade
upgrade.description: A more complete engagement that combines strategy and execution to refine the product itself and improve how it is presented.
upgrade.bestFor: Best for founders who want the product and presentation to match the ambition
included: Landing page improvement or new conversion-focused landing
Clearer product presentation and stronger trust signals across the experience

steps.title: Audit
steps.description: I review the product, identify the highest-impact issues, and surface what is hurting clarity, trust, speed, and conversion.

steps.title: Prioritize
steps.description: We define the most important fixes for the current stage of the product. No vague roadmaps. Just focused priorities.

steps.title: Refine
steps.description: I improve the product where it matters most: structure, polish, interactions, performance, and conversion-critical flows.

steps.title: Deliver
steps.description: You get a version that feels clearer, stronger, faster, and more credible without turning the whole project into a rebuild.

about.title: About
about.imageAlt: Luis Rojas — software engineer and LaunchRefined founder
about.p1: I’m Luis Rojas, a software engineer with 10+ years of experience building and improving real-world applications used by thousands of users.
about.p2: Most validated MVPs don’t need a rebuild — they need sharper UX, better performance, and stronger trust signals. That’s the gap I focus on.
about.p3: You work directly with me. No layers, no handoffs, no bloated process — just focused execution on what actually improves the product.

typicalIssues.title: Typical issues I fix
items: Generic landing experiences that undersell the product

items.question: Do you only work with Base44 products?
items.answer: No. I also work with products built with Lovable, Bolt, v0, and other modern stacks. Base44 is simply one of the clearest examples of this market.

items.question: Do you rebuild products from scratch?
items.answer: No. LaunchRefined is focused on refinement, not full rebuilds. The goal is to improve what already works.

items.question: Do you fix performance issues too?
items.answer: Yes. Performance, responsiveness, and perceived speed are part of the refinement process because they directly affect trust and conversion.

items.question: Do you also create landing pages?
items.answer: Yes. The Validated MVP Upgrade can include landing improvement or a new conversion-focused landing.

items.question: How do you define scope?
items.answer: Scope is defined around the highest-impact screens, flows, and issues for the current stage of the product. This keeps engagements focused and efficient.

items.question: Do you offer ongoing support?
items.answer: Possibly, after a successful sprint. The priority is first to improve the product through a focused engagement.

items.question: What happens after I apply?
items.answer: I review your submission, assess fit, and reply with next steps. If it is a strong fit, we move into an audit or a scoped sprint conversation.

apply.title: Apply
apply.description: Fill out a short request and I’ll review whether an Audit, a Sprint, or the full Upgrade is the right fit.
